tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The Great White Shark is one of the largest and most powerful predatory fish in the ocean.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: It has a streamlined body, sharp teeth and strong swimming ability which makes it a top ocean predator.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Great white sharks can detect tiny vibrations in water and locate prey from far distances.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Octopuses are intelligent marine animals known for their eight flexible arms and ability to change color.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They can camouflage themselves and escape predators by releasing ink into the water.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Octopuses are considered one of the most intelligent invertebrates in the ocean.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Dolphins are highly intelligent marine mammals known for their playful behavior and social groups.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They use echolocation to locate fish and navigate through water.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Dolphins communicate with whistles and clicks and are known to help injured members of their group.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Jellyfish are soft-bodied marine animals known for their umbrella-shaped bell and long tentacles.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They use stinging cells on their tentacles to capture prey and defend themselves.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Some jellyfish species glow in the dark due to bioluminescence.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Seahorses are small marine fish known for their unique horse-like head shape.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They swim upright and use their tails to hold onto sea plants.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: In seahorses the male carries the eggs in a pouch until they hatch.
